programme.title: Wild Thailand
programme.desc: Nature finds a way of living alongside people in central Thailand's forests, fertile plains and city streets.

programme.title: Wild Thailand
programme.desc: In the jungles of Phu Khieo live the leaf monkeys, unusual primates that eat the leaves of a hundred different types of plant.

programme.title: Wild New Zealand
programme.desc: Untouched since the dawn of time, evolution runs riot in New Zealand to create some of the rarest and most unusual wildlife on Earth.

programme.title: Wild New Zealand
programme.desc: Male sea lions brawl for mating rights and females give birth on a sandy beach in the Auckland Islands, located halfway between New Zealand and Antarctica.

programme.title: Wild New Zealand
programme.desc: Four penguin species begin breeding every winter in four very different and rugged locations on New Zealand's South Island.

programme.title: Wild Japan
programme.desc: The central island of Honshu is home to over 100 million people, but is also home to an astonishing range of wildlife.

programme.title: Wild Japan
programme.desc: In the far south of Japan, there is a a place where all life is influenced by the power of the sea, and where volcanoes and typhoons are forces to be reckoned with.

programme.title: Wild Japan
programme.desc: Japan's northernmost island, Hokkaido is a place totally unlike the rest of the country. Every year it swings from a bitter Siberian winter into the warmth of a Mediterranean-like summer.

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The riches of the Caribbean and the processes that shape them.

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: An exploration of the stunning underwater treasures of the Caribbean Sea, including the world's second largest barrier reef and countless shipwrecks.

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The survival stories of the islands' wildlife residents following the Caribbean hurricane season.

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The wild shore of Central America is the Caribbean's more mysterious side, with beautiful archipelagos of hundreds of islands and wildlife.

programme.title: Amazon Abyss
programme.desc: A team of scientists search for new species at the bottom of the Amazon river.

programme.title: Amazon Abyss
programme.desc: Mike deGruy and the team find a fish that talks.

programme.title: Amazon Abyss
programme.desc: The divers and scientists reach the halfway point of their 2000-mile expedition.

programme.title: Amazon Abyss
programme.desc: The team of divers and scientists arrive at the very deepest part of the jungle.

programme.title: Amazon Abyss
programme.desc: Embarking on an extraordinary journey deep into the heart of a dangerous and beautiful world.

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Something strange in the water in the Caribbean; a four-foot worm in a fish tank; a tick turns America vegetarian.

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: A crab is milked for its blue blood; stones move by themselves; thousands of rabbits chase down tourists.

programme.title: Global News National
programme.desc: Providing Canadians with the day’s top international, national, and local news stories, 24-hours a day, 365 days/week.

programme.desc: What's happening across Canada and around the world.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth and original journalism; Adrienne Arsenault and Ian Hanomansing anchor.

programme.title: CBC News: Morning
programme.desc: What do you need to know about the world around you? Heather Hiscox takes the helm as the anchor for this weekday-morning news program to tell you. Joining her is Colleen Jones with weather and sports info., and Danielle Bochove with all you need to know about the business world.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: Today's Top Stories
programme.desc: What's happening across Canada and around the world.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: Today's Top Stories

programme.title: W5
programme.desc: W5 investigates the identity of a would-be bank robber who exploded in a dramatic heist.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Natural ways to treat infertility; saving money.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Actor John Lithgow; Jonathan and Drew Scott.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Dr. Marla Shapiro's battle over breast cancer; Nadine Mercey and Brian Daly ("Paranormal Homes Investigation").

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Adrian Grenier ("Entourage"); The Toronto International Film Festival red carpet; Bryce Wylde.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Sprucing up your home; Owen Reeves.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Washing laundry; real estate guru Scott McGillivray.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: CTV News anchor Lisa LaFlamme.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Lynda Reeves; tech expert Nicholas Montgomery.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Chef Rocco Dispirito; hair stylist Marc Anthony.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: A family affected by the tornado in Goderich gets a complete makeover.

programme.desc: Natural ways to treat infertility; saving money.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Host Jessica McGovern whips up some sweet treats that are perfect for a summer pool party.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Jessica bakes desserts inspired by the colours and flavours of spring.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Host Jessica McGovern bakes up some delicious treats using autumn-inspired ingredients.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Host Jessica McGovern makes sweet winter treats like an extravagant Gingerbread Chalet.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Flour Power is a fun, stylish and playful series that is all about sharing the love and joy of baking.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Jessica makes fair-inspired desserts, including classic funnel cake.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Host Jessica McGovern creates her favourite sweet and salty treats.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Host Jessica McGovern bakes delicious desserts for a galaxy-inspired party.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Vanessa Gianfrancesco teaches us how to make fresh pasta like an Italian.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Award-winning Argentinian chef Natalia Machado perfects a line-up of dishes featuring cheese.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Italy's Love Affair with The Tomato
programme.desc: The humble tomato – called the pomodoro, or ‘apple of gold’ in Italy – is the star of this week's dish.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Natalia Machado knows that land well, and she's making recipes that come from Patagonia.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: One cannot live on bread alone, but you sure can try, and Italian chef Vanessa Gianfrancesco is going to show us how.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Italian Dishes that will Impress any Dinner Guest
programme.desc: Chef Vanessa Gianfrancesco knows just how to make Polpetti, little Italian meatballs, to perfection.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Award-winning Argentinian chef Natalia Machado shows us some of the many delicious ways to use corn in recipes, starting off with tamales.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Green beans ranch-style is on the menu as Argentinian chef Natalia Machado shows us how to make this delicious dish of green beans and eggs.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Why order delicate fried zucchini flowers at a posh restaurant when we can make them so easily in our own kitchen?

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Award-winning Argentinian chef Natalia Machado has a personal connection to ensalada de palmitos y camarones, or palm heart & shrimp salad.

programme.title: The Good Stuff with Mary Berg
programme.desc: Mary’s Caprese Pasta Salad; stylish spring looks for work; coastal décor with Colin & Justin; 'One Golden Summer' author Carley Fortune.

programme.title: The Good Stuff with Mary Berg
programme.desc: Two avid campers and home cooks compete in the Great Canadian Camping Cookoff with chef Michael Smith; glamping must-haves; RV travel.

programme.title: The Good Stuff with Mary Berg
programme.desc: Mary’s wardrobe confessionals; garage sale tips to get top dollar; dim sum lesson; Academy Award winner Julianne Moore.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Make your own checkered tablecloth magic with Mary’s recipes for Italian-American restaurant night.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Hosted by chef and two-time Canadian Screen Award-winner Mary Berg, follows Berg as she takes viewers step-by-step through recipes.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Impress pals with recipes that are great to make and easier to take, and they just might steal the show at the next potluck.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary shows how to up the baking game without breaking the bank on fancy gadgets. There’s no specialty equipment here!

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary keeps shopping lists short with flavourful dishes made up of just a few ingredients.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Celebrate the veg life with Mary’s meatless recipes that are so much more than just a bowl of greens.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary shakes up a cocktail of recipes that use beer and wine to load up on flavor.

programme.title: Faster Than Light: the Dream of Interstellar Flight
programme.desc: Next-generation rocket technology is fueling the dream of traveling across the void.

programme.title: Our World is on Fire
programme.desc: Climate change is now a crisis, and our world is crying out. Examine some possible remedies to the emergency.

programme.title: Day of the Asteroid
programme.desc: Armed with new defensive technologies, scientists are getting ready for the Day of the Asteroid.

programme.title: Searching for Planet 9
programme.desc: Is there a "Planet 9" five times more massive than planet Earth? There's fascinating new research out there.

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.desc: Over time, the Atlantic Ocean widened. Then Africa moved north, struck Europe and birthed the Alps.

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.desc: Gondwana, the first, single, landmass broke apart into today’s continents, but its heart was Africa.

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.desc: Volcanic activity and climate change define Africa’s modern history.

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: Relive the harrowing moments in Launch Control during NASA's second "Return to Flight" test flight .

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: As the clock ticks toward the critical Mars launch window, NASA scrambles to fix a critical space mistake.

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: Despite multiple pressures, a shocking discovery causes NASA to cancel a historic launch.

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: A spacecraft freefalls from orbit smashing into the ground when its parachute fails to deploy.

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: A harrowing space mistake leaves the solid rocket boosters hanging by a thread.

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: The loss of Space Shuttle Columbia stands as one of the worst disasters in spaceflight history.

programme.title: Hubble: Eye in the Sky
programme.desc: In this beautiful show, viewers get an inside look at the challenges of operating the Hubble telescope.

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.sub-title: Asia: The Tectonics of Life and Death
programme.desc: First Asia hurtled into Europe. They remain bound together by the Urals.

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.sub-title: Asia: Rising Mountains and Sinking Countries
programme.desc: As the Indo-Asian collision continued into the rest of Asia, the deforming pressure created islands.

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.desc: One billion years ago there was just one great continent on earth. Then, it began to break apart.

programme.title: Order and Disorder
programme.desc: Jim investigates a strange set of laws that link together everything from engines to humans to stars.

programme.title: Order and Disorder
programme.desc: Jim learns that information isn't just about human communication; it's woven into the very fabric of reality.

programme.title: The Age of Hubble
programme.desc: The Hubble Space Telescope has delivered unprecedented discoveries about the nature of our Universe, and more.

programme.title: Dust Storm
programme.desc: We investigate dust storm causes, and how to predict them and lessen their impact.

programme.title: Solar Superstorms: Journey to the Center of the Sun
programme.desc: As technology and electrical grids have spread, big solar storms pose a threat equal to the worst hurricanes.

programme.title: Asteroid Mining: The New El Dorado
programme.desc: Our recent rise in consumption has fueled a need for rare metals, but where can we find enough?

programme.title: China's Long March Into Space
programme.desc: Three men sit atop 500 tons of liquid fuel in China's Shenzhou 7 ship, ready to blast 185 miles into space.

programme.title: The Jupiter Enigma
programme.desc: NASA’s Juno mission to Jupiter is revealing the origins of our solar system, when Earth's fate was uncertain.

programme.title: Our Roots, Our Future
programme.desc: Scientists are studying root growth. Roots may help solve climate change and protect the environment.

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.desc: Over time, the Atlantic Ocean widened. Then Africa moved north, struck Europe and birthed the Alps.

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.desc: Gondwana, the first, single, landmass broke apart into today’s continents, but its heart was Africa.

programme.title: Antarctica
programme.desc: Explore Antarctica, one of the world's most beautiful, yet inhospitable, landscapes.

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: An asteroid could be barreling toward Earth at any time, now or in the future. Only science can save us.

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Tornadoes: Can we use modern technology to predict the emergence and path of this violent force of nature?

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Volcanoes: How successful can we be in the fight against this violent, destructive, elemental force of nature?

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Melting ice at Earth's poles: Do we still have a chance to stop it from melting and prevent its repercussions?

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: Take an exciting journey of discovery into an almost untouched wilderness: The Jura Mountains.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: This episode focuses on Ticino, reaching from the Tessin Alps down to the charming Muggio Valley.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: Take an amazing journey from the highest peaks of the Bernese Alps to a spectacular finish at the Rhine Falls.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: The ibex, marmot, and European adder have all developed strategies to survive the harsh alpine winter.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: Volcanoes can bury entire cities in clouds of ash and gas, or turn rivers into torrents of mud and debris.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: There is no stopping these massive and deadly phenomena, the only thing to do is run to higher ground.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: From the tremendous, to the terrifying, to the tragic, this is a countdown to a mega tornado.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: Earthquakes baffle experts as they try to gather the information necessary to predict the next big one.

programme.title: Earth in 1000 years
programme.desc: Earth's stores of ice on Greenland and Antarctica are melting, and sea levels rising. Where is Earth headed?

programme.title: Antarctica
programme.desc: Explore Antarctica, one of the world's most beautiful, yet inhospitable, landscapes.

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: An asteroid could be barreling toward Earth at any time, now or in the future. Only science can save us.

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Tornadoes: Can we use modern technology to predict the emergence and path of this violent force of nature?

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Volcanoes: How successful can we be in the fight against this violent, destructive, elemental force of nature?

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Melting ice at Earth's poles: Do we still have a chance to stop it from melting and prevent its repercussions?

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: Take an exciting journey of discovery into an almost untouched wilderness: The Jura Mountains.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: This episode focuses on Ticino, reaching from the Tessin Alps down to the charming Muggio Valley.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: Take an amazing journey from the highest peaks of the Bernese Alps to a spectacular finish at the Rhine Falls.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: The ibex, marmot, and European adder have all developed strategies to survive the harsh alpine winter.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: The West Coast of Madagascar is remote and inhospitable but home to an astonishing array of animals.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: The Northeast of Madagascar is a major trading center and one of the most species-rich regions in the world.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: The Secret of Migrating Birds - Tryst at the Red Sea
programme.desc: Each year in spring, Israel witnesses a unique spectacle. Many millions of migrating birds cross the land bridge between Africa, Asia and Europe.Their first major roosting place after an exhausting nonstop flight over the African deserts is in southern Israel – a vital stopover. Here they replenish their energy reserves. The flocks of migrating birds are pursued by flocks of ornithologists. Hundreds of birders flood into the port city of Eilat on the Red Sea to take part in the Migration Festival. For a week, Israel's southernmost coastal resort is home to international scientists and amateur birders.

programme.title: Down to Earth - Flightless Birds
programme.desc: They’ve given up the wonderful art of flying. Or was it an art they never possessed? The brain structure and anatomy of flightless birds indicate that they descended from "flyers" – so, why don’t they fly?

programme.title: Penguin City - South Georgia
programme.desc: In the middle of the Antarctic Ocean, an entire mountain range arises from the water: South Georgia, the nursery of the Antarctic. Hundreds of thousands of penguins, elephant seals, fur seals and their young overcrowd the beaches. The rough weather and the extremely difficult access to the island cause filmmaking to be an endeavor requiring much of the film crew around Roland Gockel and Rosie Koch and the state of the art cameras. A lot of patience and sensitivity over a period spanning some five years now offers unknown and poignant insights into the life of the King Penguins on South Georgia Island

programme.title: Germany's Wild Birds
programme.desc: By monitoring ten interesting bird-watching locations, we document not only the silver herons in the Westerwald, but also the last great bustards in Havelland. Then there are the great crested grebes in their splendid plumages, shown building their nests and during courtship, in an area of extinct volcanoes: the Eiffel. Add to this, exotic wild birds and colorful parakeets, the newest members of the population in a park in Wiesbaden. We have resting migratory birds, ducks and waders on the west banks of the Steinhuder Lake. On the border to the Netherlands, in the moorlands near Münster, we find the only wild flamingos in Germany. Equally interesting are the fire geese, avocets and many birds of the coastal region not far from Hamburg harbor. At Federsee Lake in Upper Swabia, we observe the inhabitants of the extensive reed zones: bearded tits. Then we have the birds of fortune, the cranes, which interrupt their long journeys by resting on the Baltic coast. Last, but not least, in the Feldberg lake region we accompany the ranger on an exciting sea eagle excursion. With this film, you are invited to experience a journey through the wonderful world of birds.

programme.title: Cuckoo - The Enemy in My Nest
programme.desc: It’s no news that cuckoos are brood parasites: they lay their eggs into the nests of other bird species – a clever trick that spares the rearing of own offspring. Some even go as far as to kill the other chicks in the nest. But why do other parent birds let this happen? Are they too stupid? Too inept? Or simply clueless? Admittedly, cuckoo’s eggs do look deceptively genuine. But the cuckoo chick? Not even remotely! It quickly grows into a giant that looks nothing like its new family. So why don’t the adoptive parents notice the sham then? Until recently, the ornithologists’ theory was: the “victims” have yet to learn to counteract. Turns out: they may even profit from having strangers’ chicks in their nest! So is cuckoo’s behaviour a phenomenal trick or an evolutionary riddle?

programme.title: Bosnia Herzegovina
programme.desc: Wild and unknown lies the still undiscovered natural paradise of Bosnia and Herzegovina in the heart of Europe. Rough mountains, dense forests and wild waterfalls characterize the picture of the untouched landscapes. Lynx, wolves and bears roam mountains and valleys. One of the last primeval forests in Europe is a retreat for many endangered animal species. Bosnia and Herzegovina is actually an undiscovered natural paradise in the middle of Europe.

programme.title: Bosnia Herzegovina
programme.desc: The barren, karstic south of the country, Herzegovina, also impresses with its natural landscapes. One of them is the Livno field. Not only is it the largest wetland in the country, it is also the largest regularly flooded karst field in the world. About 700 wild horses live in this unique landscape. Their ancestors were working horses that were abandoned here more than 50 years ago. Another special feature of the karst landscape are the rivers with their tufa barriers. The countless waterfalls that pour over it are a unique natural phenomenon.

programme.title: Wilderness in Europe: The Bohemian Forest
programme.desc: Pavel Hubeny, whose life would be unthinkable without the forest, is the head of the Šumava National Park. He is committed to ensuring that a primeval forest can grow for future generations, in which man does not interfere. This vision of a primeval forest is also shared by the forester Claus Bässler, who wants to counteract the extinction of species by having trees felled to create artificial deadwood and thus a new habitat for rare beetles and fungi. On the Vltava River lies the small town of Krumlov, the "Pearl of the Bohemian Forest" with its Baroque theater and fully preserved stage technology from the 17th century. Here the director Zuzana Vrbová enchants people from all over the world with her productions. Apart from agriculture, it was mainly the abundance of forests that ensured the livelihood of the inhabitants of the Bohemian Forest - including that of the carpenter Philipp von Manz, who processed local wood for his customers all over Germany. Veterinarian Judith Gollner, who grew up in the Austrian part of the Bohemian Forest, lived in Vienna for a long time and made a conscious decision to return. She likes the people and is proud of "her farmers' wives" who share her love of the Bohemian Forest.

programme.title: Wilderness in Europe: The Bohemian Forest
programme.sub-title: The Psychogramm of a Spiritual Landscape
programme.desc: The Bohemian Forest is the source of the Vltava River, home to primeval forests, untouched high moors and bubbling torrents. This archaic stretch of land, which includes the Bavarian Forest, was long considered impenetrable and mysterious. Since the fall of the Iron Curtain, Bavarian border residents have rediscovered new references to this unusual forest landscape for themselves. This is also the case with photo artist Bastian Kalous, who has been documenting his homeland for years with nostalgic-looking Polaroid photographs and is dealing with its eventful history for an illustrated book. Or Elisa Belotti and Marco Heurich, who have dedicated their lives to the lynxes. In order for Europe's largest wild cat to survive in the Bohemian Forest, the lynx researchers fight for every single animal. Iryna Rudenko came to Zwiesel from Ukraine out of a passion for glass as a material to study at the glass school. After graduation, she would like to stay in Bavaria, Anna Hones grew up in Horská Kvilda, the smallest and highest village in the Czech Republic. The village lies just under 1100 meters above sea level, directly on the former border. The farmer, who experienced communism, the fall of the Iron Curtain and the subsequent changes, is now trying to find ways of surviving in this barren region with her children.

programme.title: Wild Bavaria
programme.desc: The moon disappears behind the horizon and the stage is set for a new day in the Alps of Berchtesgaden.

programme.title: Wild Bavaria
programme.sub-title: Wild Bavaria - In The Shadow of the Watzmann.
programme.desc: Mighty and mysterious, King Watzmann sits above Germany's only alpine national park.

programme.title: Wild River Rhine
programme.desc: Alluvial forests, roaring waterfalls, steep canyons and sunny slopes flank the river Rhine. The variety of habitats is the reason for the large biodiversity along the stream.There is hardly any other river in Germany that is surrounded by as many myths and legends as the river Rhine. No other river has been the topic of songs, has been painted and travelled equally frequently. Despite this it still seems to be true today what the French poet Victor Hugo once said about this river – the Rhine is a river everybody talks about, nobody explores, everybody visits but no one really knows. The film follows the water against the stream. It starts at the river’s delta in the Netherlands, flows through six countries and ends in the Swiss Alps.

programme.title: The Moor
programme.desc: Moors and peatlands are oases in the cultivated landscape of Central Europe.

programme.title: Treasure of Lima - Buried Exhibition
programme.desc: This is a story about a remote island in the pacific.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: The Parque Nacional Peneda-Gerês is the only national park in Portugal. An area of almost 700 square kilometers, located in the extreme north of Portugal. Barren and rocky ridges in the plateaus, contrast with large forests and lush ground vegetation in deep valleys, crossed by lakes and rivers. The park is known for its oak forests, which are predominantly pedunculate and Pyrenean oaks. Among the typical animals of the park are the small Garrano ponies, a very old breed of horse and the only one in Europe that still lives in the wild.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: The highest mountain range in Portugal is the Serra da Estrela. The episode embarks on a voyage of discovery to adventurous rock formations and villages steeped in tradition. The nature park is the largest protected area on the Iberian peninsula. In the mountain range we accompany the young scientist Ana Matos, who wants to breathe new life into old traditions, and cheese maker Maria Natália Lopes. She watches over more than 350 sheeps.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.sub-title: South West Portugal: Alentejano and Costa Vicentina
programme.desc: In the southwest of Portugal lies the little known Parque Natural do Sudoeste Alentejano e Costa Vicentina. It encompasses a narrow coastal strip that runs along the coast to the south of Portugal. Sparsely populated, this coastal region, which was placed under protection in 1988, is considered one of the last intact ecosystems in Europe. The steep cliffs, the almost constant wind and high waves have ensured that mass tourism has not gained a foothold here. There are still near-natural forests and a diverse bird life.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: Madeira - Portugal's autonomous island in the Atlantic Ocean, is closer to the African coast than to the mother country Portugal, from which it is separated by about 900 kilometers. The island's name comes from Portuguese sailors who claimed the uninhabited isle for themselves in 1419. Madeira means wood and that was available in abundance. Almost the entire surface was covered by dense forest. Since 1982, two-thirds of the landscape has been protected.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: The "Parque Natural da Ria Formosa" is one of the largest lagoon conservation areas in Europe. Bordered by five natural islands. A mosaic of sandbanks, brackish and freshwater lagoons and salt marshes. In 2010, the "Ria Formosa" was chosen as one of the seven natural wonders of Portugal. It is a unique ecosystem: in the winter months, more than 20,000 birds gather here. In addition, the nature park has one of the largest seahorse populations internationally. More than half of all fish species in the Atlantic are said to spawn in the lagoon.

programme.title: Symphony of the Seas - Underwater Wonders of Australia's Ocean
programme.desc: Our journeys take us all around the world from Patagonia to the USA, from Greenland to South Africa and from Cambodia to Australia and leads us to the most fascinating places where landscapes animals and humans are the main focus of our documentaries. Whether on land or at sea – the last paradises of the world are our destination.

programme.title: Bosnia Herzegovina
programme.desc: Wild and unknown lies the still undiscovered natural paradise of Bosnia and Herzegovina in the heart of Europe. Rough mountains, dense forests and wild waterfalls characterize the picture of the untouched landscapes. Lynx, wolves and bears roam mountains and valleys. One of the last primeval forests in Europe is a retreat for many endangered animal species. Bosnia and Herzegovina is actually an undiscovered natural paradise in the middle of Europe.

programme.title: Bosnia Herzegovina
programme.desc: The barren, karstic south of the country, Herzegovina, also impresses with its natural landscapes. One of them is the Livno field. Not only is it the largest wetland in the country, it is also the largest regularly flooded karst field in the world. About 700 wild horses live in this unique landscape. Their ancestors were working horses that were abandoned here more than 50 years ago. Another special feature of the karst landscape are the rivers with their tufa barriers. The countless waterfalls that pour over it are a unique natural phenomenon.

programme.title: Wilderness in Europe: The Bohemian Forest
programme.desc: Pavel Hubeny, whose life would be unthinkable without the forest, is the head of the Šumava National Park. He is committed to ensuring that a primeval forest can grow for future generations, in which man does not interfere. This vision of a primeval forest is also shared by the forester Claus Bässler, who wants to counteract the extinction of species by having trees felled to create artificial deadwood and thus a new habitat for rare beetles and fungi. On the Vltava River lies the small town of Krumlov, the "Pearl of the Bohemian Forest" with its Baroque theater and fully preserved stage technology from the 17th century. Here the director Zuzana Vrbová enchants people from all over the world with her productions. Apart from agriculture, it was mainly the abundance of forests that ensured the livelihood of the inhabitants of the Bohemian Forest - including that of the carpenter Philipp von Manz, who processed local wood for his customers all over Germany. Veterinarian Judith Gollner, who grew up in the Austrian part of the Bohemian Forest, lived in Vienna for a long time and made a conscious decision to return. She likes the people and is proud of "her farmers' wives" who share her love of the Bohemian Forest.

programme.title: Wilderness in Europe: The Bohemian Forest
programme.sub-title: The Psychogramm of a Spiritual Landscape
programme.desc: The Bohemian Forest is the source of the Vltava River, home to primeval forests, untouched high moors and bubbling torrents. This archaic stretch of land, which includes the Bavarian Forest, was long considered impenetrable and mysterious. Since the fall of the Iron Curtain, Bavarian border residents have rediscovered new references to this unusual forest landscape for themselves. This is also the case with photo artist Bastian Kalous, who has been documenting his homeland for years with nostalgic-looking Polaroid photographs and is dealing with its eventful history for an illustrated book. Or Elisa Belotti and Marco Heurich, who have dedicated their lives to the lynxes. In order for Europe's largest wild cat to survive in the Bohemian Forest, the lynx researchers fight for every single animal. Iryna Rudenko came to Zwiesel from Ukraine out of a passion for glass as a material to study at the glass school. After graduation, she would like to stay in Bavaria, Anna Hones grew up in Horská Kvilda, the smallest and highest village in the Czech Republic. The village lies just under 1100 meters above sea level, directly on the former border. The farmer, who experienced communism, the fall of the Iron Curtain and the subsequent changes, is now trying to find ways of surviving in this barren region with her children.

programme.title: Wild Bavaria
programme.desc: The moon disappears behind the horizon and the stage is set for a new day in the Alps of Berchtesgaden.

programme.title: Wild Bavaria
programme.sub-title: Wild Bavaria - In The Shadow of the Watzmann.
programme.desc: Mighty and mysterious, King Watzmann sits above Germany's only alpine national park.

programme.title: Wild Galapagos Series 01 Episode 02
programme.desc: home to diverse creatures, inspired Charles Darwin's evolution theory due to their unique adaptations

programme.title: Baboon Bandits
programme.desc: Meet Jimmy, Fred and Merlin, the alpha males of the Smits Baboon Gang in Cape Town, South Africa.

programme.title: Eagle - Queen Of The Skies
programme.desc: To explore the world of the eagle we follow the fortunes of one individual from hatching to leaving the nest.

programme.title: Endless Horizon
programme.desc: Namibia offers a chance to see Africa's wildlife, including elephants, lions, and hippos, in its savannahs.

programme.title: The Real Lost World
programme.desc: Follow a team of adventurers on an epic journey to the plateau of Roraima, Venezuela, to The Real Lost World.

programme.title: The Secret Lives Of Big Cats
programme.desc: Siberian tigers, the largest cats, have experienced a population crash in the last century

programme.title: The Secret Lives Of Big Cats
programme.desc: Pumas, also known as cougars or mountain lions, are more closely related to cheetahs than lions.

programme.title: Lioness in Exile
programme.desc: Manyari, the Queen, leaves her pride with her cubs to escape two young threats who want to kill them.

programme.title: Masters Of The Wild
programme.desc: Animals adapt to hostile landscapes using specialized bodies, diets, and behaviors, such as scorpions

programme.title: The Unknown Snake Of Bolivia
programme.desc: Herpetologists Juan Tims and Hiram Cuevas explore Bolivia to find venomous snakes and a new viper.

programme.title: Addos Elephants
programme.desc: Protected elephants live and thrive in Addo Elephant National Park.

programme.title: Wild Galapagos Series 01 Episode 01
programme.desc: The Galapagos Archipelago's diverse biodiversity is attributed to the Pacific's Humboldt

programme.title: Wild Galapagos Series 01 Episode 02
programme.desc: home to diverse creatures, inspired Charles Darwin's evolution theory due to their unique adaptations

programme.title: Baboon Bandits
programme.desc: Meet Jimmy, Fred and Merlin, the alpha males of the Smits Baboon Gang in Cape Town, South Africa.

programme.title: Eagle - Queen Of The Skies
programme.desc: To explore the world of the eagle we follow the fortunes of one individual from hatching to leaving the nest.

programme.title: Endless Horizon
programme.desc: Namibia offers a chance to see Africa's wildlife, including elephants, lions, and hippos, in its savannahs.

programme.title: The Real Lost World
programme.desc: Follow a team of adventurers on an epic journey to the plateau of Roraima, Venezuela, to The Real Lost World.

programme.title: Wild Faces Of Switzerland
programme.desc: Learn about four different regions of Switzerland.

programme.title: Wild Faces Of Switzerland
programme.desc: The river Aare offers important habitat for flora and fauna.

programme.title: Wild Italy: Top Predators
programme.sub-title: The Predators of the River Tiber
programme.desc: Two large water birds are experiencing an unstoppable demographic boom.

programme.title: Wild Italy: Top Predators
programme.sub-title: Mantis: The Queen of the Waterless River
programme.desc: Praying mantis, born in the dry grasslands of Southern Italy.

programme.title: Wild Italy: Top Predators
programme.desc: Peregrine falcons and Herring gull have settled in the center of Rome, now nesting in large numbers.

programme.title: Germany's Wild Wolves
programme.desc: Germany's Wild Wolves challenges modern myths about wolves, revealing their social structures

programme.title: Raccoons - The New Europeans
programme.desc: Raccoons, once brought to Germany for fur, pose a threat to Europe, with half a million living in the country

programme.title: Wild Slovakia
programme.desc: Wild Slovakia is a documentary film showcasing Slovakia's diverse wildlife, habitats, and ecosystems
